title: Acceleration
short: The rate at which velocity changes over time, measured in m/s². Caused by a net force.
full:
Acceleration is how quickly an object's velocity changes, a = Δv / Δt. Like velocity it is a vector, so an object accelerates whenever its speed changes, its direction changes, or both.

A net force is what produces acceleration: a = F / m. With no net force the velocity stays constant (Newton's first law).

The SI unit is metres per second squared (m/s²). A familiar value is g ≈ 9.81 m/s², the acceleration of free fall near Earth's surface.
